What I Teach

Topics that change
how organizations work.

Every session is built from real operating experience — not textbooks. Frameworks participants can use the next day.

01
GTM Strategy & Business Development
Your team enters new markets with a working playbook, not a prayer. Pipeline grows, sales cycles shorten, and product teams finally speak the same language as revenue teams. Built from 13 years of B2B GTM in global markets.
02
Scaling, Diagnostics & Growth Management
Your growth stops being accidental. You learn to read the business as a system — diagnose bottlenecks before they become crises, and build the architecture that survives scaling without burning out.
03
Roles, Functions & Hiring for Growth
When roles and expectations are clear, people stop waiting for instructions and stop fighting over territory. They know what they own, what to escalate, and where their zone ends. Hiring stops being a gamble and becomes a system — predictable, repeatable, scalable.
04
Business Architecture & Operating Systems
Your organization starts delivering predictable results — without depending on one person. Decisions get faster, roles become clear, and the system runs when you're not in the room.
05
Culture, Energy & the Owner's Mindset
Your team starts doing the right thing when no one is watching — not because of control, but because they're bought into the mission and the standard. Culture becomes the operating system, not the decoration.
06
Strategic Thinking Patterns
Your leaders stop reacting and start seeing the system underneath. They learn to identify root causes, read dynamics three moves ahead, and make decisions from clarity — not urgency.
How We Work Together

One session or
a full semester.

Every format is built around your audience — whether that's 30 executives or 300 students.

90 minutes – half day
Guest Lecture
A single high-impact session on one topic. Built for universities, accelerators, and corporate learning days.
  • Topic selection from any of 6 areas
  • Real cases and interactive Q&A
  • Takeaway framework for participants
  • In person or online
Most Popular
2 days – 4 weeks
Intensive Workshop
Deep-dive program where participants leave with a working system they built during the workshop.
  • Customized to your industry
  • Hands-on with real company cases
  • Group and individual exercises
  • Post-workshop Q&A support
1 – 3 months
Course Module
A structured semester module integrated into your curriculum. 2 sessions per week, exam-ready materials.
  • Full syllabus and learning objectives
  • Weekly lectures and tasks
  • Assessment design and grading
  • MBA or undergrad

About

An unusual path
to business architecture.

I started as a cryptographer. A technical education and years of working with code gave me something that stayed for life: an analytical frame. Code doesn't lie — if the logic is broken, the system fails. That way of seeing things didn't stay in IT. I started noticing that businesses have the same structure: inputs, outputs, friction points, and hidden logic that either makes everything flow or makes everything grind.

Years inside companies like Kaspersky and VMware — where things were built properly — showed me what a functioning organisation actually looks like from the inside. Then came hundreds of other companies, seen from the outside: where the same principles were missing, or applied differently, or worked even better. I started mapping it. What actually worked, stripped of everything that didn't. That became the operating methodology I teach today.

Over 7 years as a speaker and trainer — 500+ events all over the world. From small groups to conference stages with 5,000+ attendees. From 1-on-1 sessions with founders rebuilding their companies from scratch to rooms with government ministers and royalty. Every audience is a different challenge, a different language, a different set of things that need to land.

Then came TalentTech — a B2B product company at the intersection of HR and education technology. Business systems aren't just processes and org charts. They're people, culture, resistance, motivation, and the stories people tell themselves about why change isn't possible. That opened a second cycle. A psychology degree — originally pursued for client negotiations — became the foundation for everything else: how I work with teams, how I read resistance, how I design learning that actually changes behaviour.

There are no complex topics. Only teachers who haven't yet found the way to make it land for this particular person. Sometimes it takes three different approaches before something clicks. But when it does — when you see it happen in someone's eyes — that moment is worth everything that came before it. That's why I do this.

MSc Data Security & Cryptography Moscow Technical University of Communications
BSc Psychology & Conflict Negotiation Foundation for team design, coaching, and learning architecture
7+ Years Speaking & Training 500+ events worldwide · from small groups to 5,000+ attendee stages · from startup founders to government ministers and royalty
80+ Countries Enterprise operations across APAC, EMEA, LATAM, CIS
Whitewater Instructor · Category 5+ No room for assumptions or hesitation. People's lives depend on reading the system right — and acting before the current decides for you

What Clients Say

In their own words.

Anonymous by agreement — from founders, executives, and owners across different industries and company sizes.

"

Two quarters of flat revenue. The team was exhausted, everyone blamed everyone else — marketing blamed product, sales blamed marketing, I blamed the market. We spent a session with Vladimir and found three real levers we'd been completely ignoring. Not the ones we thought were the problem. Eight weeks later we were growing again. The shift wasn't in the product or the team. It was in how we were reading the system. A few months after that I took a real vacation with my family for the first time in two years. That said more than any metric.

CEO
EdTech company · 80 employees · SEA
"

I grew the company to 200 people and never once stopped to restructure how decisions were actually made. Everything still went through me — every contract, every hire, every conflict. Vladimir didn't come in with a framework and lecture me. He showed me what was actually happening in the system I had built with my own hands. I wasn't scaling the business. I was becoming its biggest bottleneck. We redesigned the decision architecture over several sessions. Six months later I was spending my time on strategy and new markets instead of firefighting daily operations. What struck me most was his ability to take something that felt genuinely complex and impossible to explain — and make it feel obvious.

Founder
Logistics group · 200+ employees · Russia/CIS
"

We had serious churn in our teaching team — good people kept leaving within three months and we couldn't figure out why. We were hiring on gut feel, interviewing for personality, and crossing our fingers. There was no role architecture, no clear accountability, no system for onboarding. Vladimir walked us through something that felt almost too simple to work: design the role first, then build the hiring system around it, then find the person. Retention went from 52% to 85% in four months. Same budget, same market, same type of candidates. We didn't find better people. We built a better structure — and then hired the right people into it. The order matters more than anything.

COO
Online education platform · B2C · 25 employees
"

I own a small business — car washes and auto protection. I was convinced that corporate frameworks had nothing to do with my world. Vladimir and I are friends, and one day we argued about it. We decided to find out. Two weeks in, we surfaced the secondary gains behind my avoidance — reasons I hadn't seen, and hadn't been honest with myself about. Four weeks in, I stopped believing my business was somehow special and exempt from the rules. Three months in: we raised prices 20% and didn't lose a single client. Zero churn. But what surprised me most was the team — same people, completely different behavior. More initiative, more thinking before acting, higher ownership. By the end of the program I had a 6-month implementation plan I built myself — with Vladimir's guidance, but with my own hands. I'm in month four now. We move carefully because in a small team, fast change means chaos and people leaving — and unlike big companies, we can't afford that kind of disruption. But I know the real changes are coming. Some are already here — and there will be more. They can't not come. I know that now.

Owner
Car wash & auto protection · under 10 people · Russia
